answer: Rust is generally considered safer than C++ for memory safety because it enforces ownership and borrowing rules at compile time.
keyClaims: Rust enforces memory safety through compile-time ownership rules.
C++ allows manual memory management which can introduce memory errors.
Modern C++ can mitigate some safety risks but does not enforce ownership like Rust.
assumptions: The comparison focuses on memory safety rather than performance or ecosystem.
We are comparing typical usage patterns rather than expert-level defensive C++ coding.
